hero.title: Building the leaders
hero.subtitle: A private program helping young people aged 12 to 22 grow in confidence, discipline and leadership.

manifesto.body: chools teach subjects. At Mentou, we teach young people how to become themselves. Tutoring, reading, expression, movement and mentorship come together in a single program designed to help every student find their voice, confidence and direction.
manifesto.quote: Much more than tutoring: a complete program to grow with assurance.

whoItsFor.title: For young people who need structure to reveal themselves
whoItsFor.body: Mentou is for families who feel there is more to build than a report card: posture, voice, discipline, and direction.

items.title: The bright but reserved young person
items.body: They understand quickly, but do not always dare to take their place, ask questions, or defend their ideas.

items.title: The teen who lacks structure
items.body: They have potential, but need strong habits, a clear rhythm, and adults who hold the course with them.

items.title: The ambitious but scattered student
items.body: They want to succeed, but must learn to prioritize, communicate, organize, and turn energy into progress.

items.title: The family looking for more than tutoring
items.body: They want holistic support: academic, human, cultural, and personal, in a demanding and caring environment.

items.title: Academic Tutoring
items.body: Targeted academic support to do your best in high school and CEGEP, and prepare calmly for what comes next. Our tutors adapt each session to the student's pace and needs, with clear and measurable goals.
items.practice: Focused sessions around homework, exams, and study methods, with a clear goal for each meeting.

items.title: Strategic Reading
items.body: A curated selection of meaningful books — finance, habits, strategy, communication — we read together and apply daily. Each book becomes a concrete tool to think differently and act more intelligently.
items.practice: Guided reading, discussion of strong ideas, and concrete application in school, social, or family life.

items.title: Educational Debates
items.body: Conversations and debates to grow general culture, critical thinking and the ease to express yourself. Students learn to defend their ideas, listen to others, and build solid arguments.
items.practice: Debates, short position statements, and listening exercises to learn to think before answering.

items.title: The Art of Sketch
items.body: Improvisation and short scenes to gain confidence, settle your voice and become comfortable in front of others. Through play, students develop a natural presence and learn to feel at ease in all social situations.
items.practice: Improvisations, short scenes, and voice exercises to become comfortable under the gaze of others through play.

items.title: Dance & Presence
items.body: Working on posture, rhythm and body language to feel good in your skin and naturally inspire confidence. Dance teaches a fundamental truth: the body is the first vehicle of leadership — before any words.
items.practice: Work on rhythm, posture, and coordination to develop a more confident physical presence.

items.title: Musical Mastery
items.body: Learning an instrument strengthens patience, listening and the ability to progress a little each day. Music teaches a fundamental truth: consistent discipline produces extraordinary results.
items.practice: Progressive work with an instrument or musical exercise to train focus, patience, and consistency.

items.title: Mentorship & Culture
items.body: Personal mentoring to clarify goals, and an opening to world cultures to grow with curiosity and respect. A mentor is someone who has already walked a path and helps you find your own.
items.practice: Guided conversations, personal goals, and cultural discovery to help the young person name what they want to build.

programRhythm.title: A week at Mentou
programRhythm.body: The program combines academic work, expression, culture, and mentorship in a clear rhythm. Every activity serves a visible goal: learn better, speak better, stand taller, choose better.

facts.label: Rhythm
facts.note: A weekly volume designed to progress without overwhelming school, family, and other commitments.

facts.label: Cohorts
facts.note: Intentionally small groups to keep support human and conversations meaningful.

facts.label: Formats
facts.note: Tutoring, reading, public speaking, movement, music, and mentorship alternate according to the path.

facts.label: Follow-up
facts.note: Families stay involved through check-ins and concrete markers of the young person's progress.

transformation.title: What changes for those who join Mentou

divisions.title: A path tailored to each stage

junior.label: Junior Cohort
junior.headline: Build confidence and find your rhythm

items.quote: Within months, my son completely changed. He dares to speak up, he's curious about new things, he found his place. Mentou gave him a framework he didn't know he was looking for.
items.author: Marie L.

items.quote: The approach is rigorous yet deeply human. Both my teens go with pleasure and come back grown each week. That's rare and precious.
items.author: Karim D.

items.quote: Before Mentou, I didn't dare speak up in class. Now, I lead a student project. The sketch exercises and debates truly unlocked something in me.
items.author: Sophia

parentsCircle.title: A program that brings parents in, too
parentsCircle.body: Moments of sharing between parents, young people and mentors. Confidence is built together, and a supportive circle makes all the difference.

admission.title: A tailored path, in four steps
admission.body: We take the time to meet every young person and every family to build support that truly fits.
admission.reassurance: First call with no commitment · quick reply

pillars.title: The Seven Pillars
pillars.subtitle: Each pillar develops an essential dimension of leadership.

engagement.title: A demanding framework, never impersonal
engagement.body: The pillars are not isolated activities. They form a guided path where each young person is observed, encouraged, and made responsible with consistency.

items.title: Track every progression
items.body: We pay attention to small victories: speaking up, keeping a routine, repeating effort, making a more mature decision.

items.title: Hold a high standard
items.body: Mentou encourages, but does not overprotect. Young people learn to honor commitments and stretch themselves without losing balance.

items.title: Involve the family
items.body: Parents remain allies in the journey. They understand what is changing and how to support the young person outside sessions.

engagement.note: The goal is not to fill a schedule. The goal is to build a trajectory.

vision.title: Schools teach.
vision.subtitle: Mentou is not academic tutoring. It is a parallel school, intimate and demanding, that prepares the next generation of Québec leaders.

manifesto.title: Today's youth need something more
manifesto.body: Schools teach you how to answer. Mentou teaches you how to question. Schools validate a grid. Mentou reveals a voice. We believe that beyond grades, what sets a future leader apart is the ability to stand tall, think independently, and turn ideas into action.
manifesto.quote: We don't train model students. We accompany young people who want to live a life that looks like them.
manifesto.author: Founder, Mentou

items.title: Confidence is built
items.body: No one is born a leader. Confidence comes from small battles won against yourself, day after day.

items.title: Environment shapes us
items.body: We become who we surround ourselves with. That's why we build a circle of ambitious, kind peers.

items.title: Discipline frees
items.body: Without rigor, talent stalls. With it, potential fully unfolds.

items.title: Mentorship changes everything
items.body: Having someone who believes in you, who sees further than you do — that's a gift that transforms a path.

method.title: A pedagogy designed like a workshop

steps.label: Observe
steps.body: Understand who the young person is, what they live, what they dream, what they avoid.

steps.label: Stimulate
steps.body: Offer the right challenges — not too easy, not crushing — to help them grow.

steps.label: Guide
steps.body: Be present over time, celebrate progress, support setbacks.

steps.label: Release
steps.body: Let the young person fly on their own once confidence is anchored.

founder.title: A project born from deep conviction
founder.name: Richard, founder of Mentou
founder.role: Mentor, framework builder, and guide for young talent
founder.body: Mentou was born from a simple observation: many young people have potential, but too few spaces to learn how to fully inhabit it. Richard wanted to create a place where grades matter, yes, but so do voice, posture, discipline, and vision.

journey.title: His journey
items: Supporting young people through academic, personal, and social challenges.
Seeing the need for a framework more complete than classic tutoring.
Building a method that connects standards, culture, expression, and mentorship.

why.title: Why Mentou?
why.body: Because a young person does not always lack ability. Sometimes they lack a circle, a rhythm, a steady adult voice, and a place where they can try, fall, begin again, and become stronger.

founder.quote: I created Mentou to offer young people what I would have loved to receive earlier: a demanding, human framework, and adults who see beyond the report card.

cta.title: Ready to join the journey?
cta.body: Let's talk about your child's path.
cta.label: Schedule an interview

admission.title: An admission that takes
admission.titleAccent: the time to do it right
admission.subtitle: We welcome a limited number of young people each year to guarantee the quality of our support. The process unfolds over a few weeks, without rushing.

process.title: Four steps, from first contact to integration

steps.label: First contact
steps.body: A phone call to understand your expectations, introduce the program, and verify that Mentou aligns with your family's vision.

steps.label: Interview
steps.body: A private meeting with the young person and parents. This is the moment to ask everything and feel if the fit is right — both ways.

steps.label: Custom plan
steps.body: We build a tailor-made support plan based on the strengths, challenges, and goals identified together.

steps.label: Cohort integration
steps.body: The young person joins their cohort (Junior or Senior) and begins their journey through Mentou's seven disciplines.

clarity.title: What you will know clearly
clarity.body: Admission is designed both to inform you and to make sure Mentou is the right environment for your young person.

items.title: The recommended rhythm
items.body: We clarify the activity volume, availability, and the best way to fit Mentou into the young person's week.

items.title: The path priorities
items.body: Confidence, method, expression, discipline, or future plans: every plan begins with simple goals.

items.title: The parents' role
items.body: You know how you will be informed, involved, and supported throughout the progression.

items.title: The practical terms
items.body: Fees, calendar, available spots, and next cohort timing are presented before any decision.

eligibility.title: Mentou is for motivated young people and their families
criteria: Young people aged 12 to 22, living in Québec
Family engaged in their child's journey
Desire to grow beyond the strict academic frame
Curiosity, openness, eagerness to push limits
eligibility.note: No selection on grades. We look for desire, not perfection.

profiles.title: Junior · 12–16
profiles.body: To build confidence, discipline, oral ease, and academic foundations at the right moment.

profiles.title: Senior · 17–22
profiles.body: To clarify a project, gain autonomy, improve methods, and prepare for the next steps.

items.a: No. Mentou complements school. Our program runs in parallel, generally at the end of the day, evenings, and weekends.

items.q: How many young people per cohort?
items.a: Our cohorts are deliberately small — between 8 and 12 — to guarantee personalized follow-up and true quality of interaction.
An annual commitment, with about 4 to 6 hours per week of structured activities. Consistency matters more than intensity.
Yes. Mentou is a private program. Fees are discussed during the interview and adapted to circumstances. Scholarships may be awarded.
We welcome new members mainly in September, with a few mid-year admissions depending on availability.

cta.title: Start the process
cta.body: First call with no commitment. We introduce ourselves, we talk, we see if the journey is right for you.
cta.label: Schedule an interview

gallery.title: Moments, looks,
gallery.subtitle: The gallery brings together meaningful moments from the program — learning, creation, workshops, stages, and conversations.

contact.title: Let's talk about
contact.subtitle: A question, a meeting, an admission request — we read every message carefully and reply within 48 hours.

form.title: Schedule an interview
form.intro: Fill out this form in a few seconds. We'll get back to you quickly through the channel that works best for you.
reassurance: First call is free and without commitment
Reply within 48 business hours, often sooner
Phone, WhatsApp or email according to your preference

nextSteps.title: After you send
items: We suggest a time for a first call
You then decide whether you want to continue

message.label: Your message
message.placeholder: Tell us about your child, their passions, what you're looking for…

form.consent: I agree to be contacted by Mentou regarding my request.
form.success: Message received. We'll reply within 48 hours.

info.title: Several ways to get in touch

items.a: Within 48 business hours, usually much faster.
Yes. The first phone call and the interview are at no cost or commitment.

items.q: Can I bring my child to the meeting?
items.a: We strongly encourage it. The meeting works best with parents and the young person together.

footer.tagline: Private leadership and personal development program.
